factors.name: US-Iran Geopolitical Tensions
factors.summary: Fresh US military strikes on Iran overnight (May 26) dimmed ceasefire hopes but subsequent reports of potential US-Iran peace deal (unconfirmed Al Jazeera) create whipsaw. Bloomberg notes gold 'held losses' as traders weighed deal prospects; tension typically supports gold but recent volatility shows weak conviction.

factors.name: Real Yields (TIPS/Fed Policy Expectations)
factors.summary: No fresh FOMC data in window, but market pricing remains anchored to sticky inflation expectations. BoJ's Ueda warned that temporary oil shocks can become persistent (May 27), signalling central banks may keep rates higher longer. This suppresses real yields and should be mildly bullish for gold, but weak price action suggests offset.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY) / Safe-Haven Flows
factors.summary: No DXY data in release window. EUR/USD trading near 1.1640, GBP/USD near 1.3450—both relatively stable. Geopolitical tensions typically support USD as safe-haven, which would weigh on gold; however, energy cost fears (Strait of Hormuz uncertainty) also support commodity prices.

factors.name: Oil Prices & Inflation Expectations
factors.summary: WTI slipped below $92.00 (down >1% on May 27) despite Middle East tensions, as traders priced in potential US-Iran peace deal and Hormuz reopening. Lower oil reduces inflation concerns and supports lower-for-longer rate narrative, which is bearish for gold's inflation-hedge premium.

factors.name: Global Central Bank Signals
factors.summary: RBNZ held OCR at 2.25% (May 27) with split 3-3 vote; Governor Breman's casting vote held, signalling hawkish tilt on future hikes. BoJ remains in easy mode per Ueda comments. Mixed signals globally; no clear tightening shock to support gold.

factors.name: China Economic Data
factors.summary: China industrial profits jumped 24.7% YoY in April (fastest in 2+ years) per official release; YTD +18.2% vs prior +15.5%. Strong data is marginally risk-positive (reduces deflation/slowdown fears) but does not directly alter gold's real-yield or inflation-expectations drivers.

factors.name: Australian CPI & AUD Impact
factors.summary: Australia April CPI came in at 4.2% YoY vs 4.4% expected (per ABS May 27), undershooting forecasts. AUD extended gains initially but later pulled back. Softer inflation in major economy supports dovish repricing and lower yields, which should be bullish for gold—but price action shows weak follow-through.

factors.name: Silver / Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: XAG/USD trading around $76.90, holding losses for second successive day despite Middle East tensions. Weak precious metals complex suggests low conviction on safe-haven bid; equities (Nikkei, SK Hynix, Samsung) rallying on chip/AI demand, indicating risk-on mood.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Physical Demand
factors.summary: No GLD, IAU, or physical demand data in news window. No seasonal tailwinds evident (no major Chinese New Year or Indian wedding season). Seasonal backdrop neutral.

factors.name: Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: Gold down -0.17% in 4h, -0.43% in 24h. Losses modest but directionally weak; held around $4,500-4,507 during Asian hours with no sharp rally despite geopolitical headlines. Suggests near-term momentum is to the downside; move is early-stage but lack of bounce is concerning for bulls.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a geopolitical-versus-inflation-expectations squeeze. Fresh US-Iran military escalation (May 26) overnight and threats of retaliation typically support safe-haven demand and commodity prices, but unconfirmed reports of a potential peace deal have undercut conviction. Simultaneously, softening inflation data (Australia CPI), lower oil prices (WTI below $92), and central bank statements (BoJ, RBNZ) suggesting rates may remain higher for longer create headwinds to real yields, which should be bullish for gold—yet the precious metal has failed to rally, instead drifting lower on low volume. This suggests weak institutional positioning and risk-on sentiment dominating (equity indices hitting records) over safe-haven flows.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran peace deal negotiations (live; potential announcement in next 12-24h)
Strait of Hormuz transit data / further geopolitical escalation
US economic calendar: Any Fed speakers (no FOMC yet in window)
PCE inflation or CPI data (if due mid-late May)
BoJ / ECB further commentary from conferences (May 27-28)
Equity earnings / risk sentiment reversal (could flip gold to safe-haven mode if markets sell off)
screening.risk_warning: A confirmed US-Iran peace deal and swift Hormuz reopening would likely trigger sharp selloff in oil and precious metals (deflation scare), crushing gold further. Conversely, escalation beyond current clashes—e.g. Iranian retaliation on US/Saudi assets—could flip sentiment sharply to safe-haven, supporting gold. Weak price action despite headlines suggests algos and short-term traders are leading; a macro surprise (hot CPI, hawkish Fed speaker) could reverse the bias quickly. Entry here is premature; wait for either a clear break below $4,490 (bearish confirmation) or a retest of $4,525+ (bullish reversal) before committing.
screening.entry_guidance: Gold is in early-stage downtrend on weak conviction. Do not chase shorts into $4,490-4,500 support yet; wait for retest of $4,525 resistance to fail before re-engaging bullish case. If geopolitical escalation headlines intensify (e.g. confirmed Iranian retaliation), expect rally to $4,550-4,570 as safe-haven bid; this would offer better technical level to take profits on any bounce. For bears, short on retest of $4,520 with stop above $4,540; target $4,480-4,470. Phase 2 conditions (low volume, directionless chop) suggest waiting 4-6 hours for clearer signal.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS data in overnight news. Fed's Kashkari emphasized inflation risk now exceeds labor market risk, suggesting hawkish tilt that would support higher real yields and weigh on gold, but this is directional bias not confirmed by new prints.

factors.name: Federal Reserve Policy
factors.summary: Kashkari (Minneapolis Fed) stated 'risk to US inflation now higher than risk of labour market deterioration' and 'most US data released since April has shown inflationary risks are higher, not lower.' This hawkish messaging and geopolitical inflationary shock from Iran war suggest Fed on hold/tightening bias, pressuring gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY trades near 99.10 per FXStreet report; USD remains supported by 'persistent geopolitical uncertainties' and safe-haven flows. Strong dollar is directly BEARISH for gold (inverse correlation).

factors.name: Geopolitical Tensions (Iran/Middle East)
factors.summary: US-Iran framework agreement talks ongoing, but fresh US strikes on southern Iran and Israeli intensification in Lebanon (31+ killed per Al Jazeera) create persistent volatility. Markets pricing in *hope* for ceasefire (oil down WTI ~$90.75) but headlines remain volatile, keeping risk premium in place.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: WTI trading ~$90.75, down on US-Iran peace optimism per FXStreet. Lower oil removes immediate inflation shock tailwind for gold and signals easing geopolitical premium; Brent also 'slips' per Bloomberg. This favors real yields staying higher and gold staying pressured.

factors.name: Inflation Data
factors.summary: Australia April CPI came in 4.2% (better than 4.4% expected) but core trimmed mean rose to 3.4% highest since late 2024; UK grocery inflation eased to 3.1%; French consumer confidence collapsed to lowest since March 2023. Disinflationary goods backdrop conflicts with core stickiness and geopolitical wage pressure.

factors.name: Other Central Banks (ECB, BoJ, RBNZ)
factors.summary: ECB policymakers signaled hawkish tilt (EUR gains on ECB hawkish sentiment). RBNZ held at 2.25% by casting vote but signaled rates will 'rise sooner and by more than expected' per investingLive; NZD surged. BoJ signals loose conditions persist but groundwork for tightening intact. Global hawkish surprise supports USD and real yields, pressuring gold.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment Regime
factors.summary: Equities show mixed resilience: S&P 500 +0.6%, Russell 2000 +1.8%, AI/chip rally (SK Hynix, Micron +$1T club) continues; but European stocks 'modest gains' and ceasefire optimism is fragile. Gold acting neither as safe-haven nor risk-off driver strongly right now.

factors.name: Gold-Specific Technical/Sentiment
factors.summary: FXStreet: 'Gold struggles near $4,500 as bulls remain on the sidelines amid hawkish rate expectations.' Psychological $4,500 level is weak support; no ETF flow data yet but lack of fresh institutional buying is notable.

factors.name: Competing Safe-Haven (USD)
factors.summary: USD remains the primary safe-haven beneficiary of geopolitical tension, with DXY near 99.10 and many pairs (EUR/USD, GBP/USD, AUD/USD) under pressure. This competes directly with gold's safe-haven appeal.

screening.narrative: Gold is trapped in a tug-of-war between hawkish Fed rhetoric (Kashkari's inflation risk upgrade) and safe-haven demand from Iran/Middle East escalation. The dominant macro story is that inflation risks are rising and real yields are likely to stay elevated or rise further, which suppresses gold. Simultaneously, oil prices have retreated on ceasefire optimism, removing near-term inflation shock premium. The US Dollar remains the primary safe-haven beneficiary, and other central banks (ECB, RBNZ) are signaling tightening ahead, which also supports USD and pressures gold. Price action is flat (-0.02% in 4h and 24h), confirming no directional conviction yet.
catalysts_ahead: US-Iran framework agreement formal announcement (expected this week per multiple sources)
Fresh Middle East military developments (ongoing Israeli-Hezbollah escalation, potential Iranian response)
Fed speakers/economic data (inflation prints, jobless claims, ISM services if due)
ECB Lagarde or other hawkish ECB commentary (further tightening bias)
Oil market reaction to Strait of Hormuz disruption risk (currently pricing in ceasefire optimism)
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran ceasefire deal is formally announced and holds, oil will likely fall further and DXY could soften on de-risking, which would flip gold BULLISH. Conversely, if fresh escalation occurs (major Iranian retaliation, Israeli action widens to Iran proper), oil spikes and geopolitical premium re-enters, supporting gold despite hawkish Fed backdrop. Additionally, if incoming CPI/PCE surprises hot, real yields could spike hard and invalidate the NEUTRAL bias entirely to downside.
screening.entry_guidance: Avoid chasing short positions into $4,500 support; price action is flat and hawkish Fed bias not yet price-accelerated. Wait for either a clear break below $4,490 (with DXY >99.30) for shorts, or a ceasefire reversal + oil spike (WTI >$93) + EUR/USD break lower before adding shorts with conviction. Long entries require real yield softening (not priced in yet) or geopolitical flare (monitor headlines closely).

factors.name: Real Yields / Fed Expectations
factors.summary: Markets now pricing ~70% chance of at least one Fed rate hike by year-end (per investinglive), a complete reversal from Dec 2025 when rate cuts were expected. Higher real yields directly pressure gold. Kashkari warns 'far too soon' to predict next move but tone remains hawkish on inflation.

factors.name: US-Iran Peace Optimism / Risk-On Sentiment
factors.summary: Persistent cautious optimism over US-Iran negotiations driving broad equity rallies (S&P 500 futures +0.3%, Nasdaq +0.4%) and lower oil prices. Risk-on flows reduce gold's safe-haven appeal. Gold explicitly struggling despite oil sell-off, breaking two-month lows near $4,450 (per FXStreet 11:31).

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY flatlines around 99.00, trading mid-range. Mixed drivers: hawkish Fed support USD, but peace talks and lower oil weaken it. No clear directional momentum; likely neutral-to-slightly-supportive for USD vs gold in this regime.

factors.name: Inflation Print / Wage Data
factors.summary: ADP weekly pulse at 35.75K vs 42.25K prior signals cooling labor market momentum. ECB's Makhlouf notes inflation concerns but 'hasn't seen second-round effects emerging.' This supports Fed hawkishness (lower near-term inflation surprise risk), reducing gold inflation hedge demand.

factors.name: Oil Prices / Hormuz Risk
factors.summary: Oil prices dropping sharply as traders optimistic on US-Iran talks progress. Deutsche Bank notes Brent retraced half of Monday's fall. Lower oil reduces inflation tail-risk and removes geopolitical premium, both headwinds for gold.

factors.name: Nominal 10y Yield Direction
factors.summary: US mortgage rates hit highest since August (per Bloomberg 11:00); JGBs seeing demand but US Treasuries broadly firm. Rising nominal yields alongside hawkish Fed messaging pressures gold despite some offsetting real yield effects.

factors.name: Equity Market Strength / Risk Sentiment
factors.summary: Equities extending records on AI optimism and de-risking Middle East. SK Hynix and Micron both hit $1T valuations; Nasdaq rising. Strong equity performance competes with gold as allocation choice; risk-on regime reduces gold safe-haven premium.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows / Physical Demand
factors.summary: No specific flow data in newsflow, but context suggests institutional flows likely negative given risk-on pivot. No seasonal tailwinds mentioned (no major Indian festival, Chinese holidays near-term).

factors.name: Geopolitical De-Risking
factors.summary: US-Iran ceasefire negotiations advancing; Strait of Hormuz uncertainty fading. Geopolitical risk premium that has supported gold is unwinding. Rabobank assumes Hormuz closes several more months, but market pricing suggests resolution coming.

factors.name: Other Central Banks (ECB, BoJ, RBNZ)
factors.summary: ECB officials (Makhlouf, Guindos) hawkish on inflation, signaling possible June hike. BoJ yield curve control steady. RBNZ kept OCR at 2.25% but signaled faster tightening ahead. Mixed signals; no clear tailwind or headwind for gold vs USD.

key_levels.support: $4,450 (two-month low, breached today per FXStreet 11:31); $4,425 (next key support)

screening.narrative: Gold is in a PHASE_2 downmove following a reversal in Fed rate expectations and broad risk-on sentiment driven by US-Iran peace talks. The headline shift from December's 'rate cuts by year-end' to 'now 70% chance of at least one hike by year-end' has eroded real yield support for gold. Simultaneously, equity markets are rallying on AI euphoria and geopolitical de-risking, pulling allocations away from the precious metal. Oil is falling, removing inflation-hedge demand and geopolitical premium. Gold has broken two-month lows despite DXY remaining neutral—a sign that real yields and risk sentiment are dominating the move, not currency flows. The -1.04% 24h decline and -1.31% 4h decline confirm the downtrend is active and not yet exhausted.
catalysts_ahead: Fed speakers (Powell, Williams, Waller, Kashkari) — any hawkish inflation commentary will extend downside; dovish pivot would reverse bias
US PCE inflation data (if released in next 48h) — critical for rate hike odds
NFP report — any strong print confirms labor market resilience, supports Fed hawkishness
US-Iran deal announcement or ceasefire breakdown — either outcome impacts oil/geopolitical premium
ECB June rate decision (if imminent) — hawkish surprise could widen rate differential, USD strength headwind for gold
Any major equity correction (VIX spike) — would snap risk-on and likely support gold safe-haven flows
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran talks collapse and Hormuz closure risk re-emerges, geopolitical premium would re-ignite gold demand and reverse the SELL bias. If incoming inflation data surprises hot (CPI/PCE > forecast), real yields could sink despite Fed hawkishness (stagflation scenario), sharply bullish for gold. If Fed pivots dovish on growth concerns, downside bias invalidated. Equity crash (>3% S&P 500) would flip gold to NEUTRAL/BUY as safe-haven demand returns.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already 1%+ in progress (PHASE_2). Avoid aggressive new shorts; instead consider short entries on any retest of $4,475–$4,490 resistance with confirmed breakdown below $4,450. If gold bounces to $4,480–$4,495 range, that offers a better risk/reward short. Watch for any Fed speaker hawkish surprise to confirm extension; otherwise, expect range-chop into weekend / early next week as catalysts queue.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD weakening modestly as markets digest conflicting US-Iran headlines; GBP/USD edges lower but EUR/USD gains 0.21% on risk-on sentiment. Weaker dollar is structurally bullish for gold, offsetting other pressures.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No new 10y TIPS yield data in this news window. Prior bias context and nominal yield direction suggest real yields likely steady to slightly elevated; insufficient data to confirm directional shift.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment & Geopolitical
factors.summary: Iran deal optimism dominating intraday: Iran presented draft MoU framework, US-Iran talks showing progress ('negotiations proceeding nicely' per Trump). Risk-on sentiment easing Hormuz closure fears, supporting equities (+0.09-0.20% opens) and reducing safe-haven demand for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Oil barges forced to carry less fuel on Rhine; Piper Sandler warns Hormuz could stay closed for months, BUT current news shows oil prices 'trim losses' and are 'softer' (per FXStreet 14:04). Easing oil = lower inflation expectations = pressure on gold.

factors.name: Inflation Data & Expectations
factors.summary: No fresh CPI/PCE/PPI data released in this window. ECB's de Guindos notes current shock = supply-driven lower growth + higher inflation (stagflationary backdrop favors gold structurally, but near-term sentiment is risk-on, not flight-to-safety).

factors.name: Fed Policy & Rate Expectations
factors.summary: No FOMC decision or new Fed speakers in this window. BBH (Elias Haddad) notes 'improving risk sentiment' and yields 'retracing' as Iran worries ease; suggests Fed hike bets not intensifying near-term.

factors.name: Equity Market & Risk Assets
factors.summary: S&P 500 +0.09%, Dow +0.20%, Nasdaq +0.14% at open; tech weakens but healthcare, consumer cyclicals rally. Risk-on tone is inversely correlated with safe-haven gold demand; equities strength typically bearish for gold in non-crisis regimes.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows & Institutional Demand
factors.summary: No GLD/IAU flow data in this window. FXStreet reports 'Gold slides to two-month lows' as 'markets balance inflation concerns and US-Iran peace hopes' (14:25), indicating weakness in institutional bids.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No new PBoC, RBI, or other CB gold purchase data in this window. Structural bid remains but not addressed in today's news flow.

factors.name: Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: Gold at 2-month lows per FXStreet (14:25). 24h change -0.72%, 4h change +0.42% (weak rebound attempt). Bearish price structure, potential breakdown below recent support.

screening.narrative: Gold is under pressure as markets shift from geopolitical risk-off into a cautious risk-on mode following Iran deal optimism (draft MoU framework presented, Trump comments 'proceeding nicely'). The Strait of Hormuz closure fears have eased slightly, oil prices are softer, and equity markets opened higher, reducing safe-haven demand. However, the USD is weakening modestly and real yields remain unclear, which normally support gold—creating a mixed backdrop. FXStreet explicitly notes gold 'slides to two-month lows' and 'struggles to attract buying interest,' suggesting institutional positioning is turning more bearish despite structural long-term gold bullishness (geopolitical tail risk, supply shocks, BoJ/ECB easing bias).
catalysts_ahead: US Jobs Report (NFP) — likely this week, major inflation/growth signal
PCE (Core & Headline) — key Fed target inflation metric, timing TBD
ECB rate decision (11 June confirmed) — 25bp hike priced in, EUR strength could weaken USD/gold carry
Further US-Iran deal updates — any breakthrough further reduces safe-haven bid
Oil Hormuz developments — if Hormuz fully reopens, crude crash likely bullish risk assets, bearish gold
screening.risk_warning: A hawkish surprise from upcoming US economic data (hot NFP, CPI beat) or Fed speakers would reverse this bias sharply; elevated real yields would crush gold despite weaker dollar. Conversely, any geopolitical escalation (Israel-Hezbollah intensifies per news, further Ukraine moves, China-Taiwan flare-up) or recession signals would flip bias back to BULLISH gold immediately. Late-stage risk-on fades quickly if deal talks stall.
screening.entry_guidance: Gold is in PHASE 2 downside (move in progress toward 2-month lows). Wait for stabilization at $4,450–$4,460 support before considering long entries; shorts should be cautious given weak USD backdrop and major data events this week could invalidate bear thesis.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY) recovery
factors.summary: DXY reversed intraday losses and recovered ground on Wednesday (per FXStreet 18:19) as traders digested conflicting US-Iran headlines. Stronger dollar directly pressures gold through inverse correlation.

factors.name: Real yields direction (implied)
factors.summary: US 5-year note auction saw yields at 4.182% vs 4.181% WI, with Treasury yields falling since May 19 highs but remaining elevated. No fresh TIPS data, but nominal yields remain sticky, limiting gold upside.

factors.name: US-Iran deal uncertainty
factors.summary: Trump explicitly rejected Iran sanctions relief and stated 'we're not satisfied with it' (16:06 timestamp). Earlier Iran MoU draft reports were denied by White House (14:25). This risk-off/USD-supportive narrative weighs on gold.

factors.name: Oil prices decline
factors.summary: Oil fell >5% after Rubio's dovish Iran comments, with Brent dropping below $100 (per Commerzbank 17:22). Lower oil reduces inflation-hedge demand for gold and eases Fed rate-hike concerns.

factors.name: Gold ETF and physical demand flows
factors.summary: No recent GLD/IAU holdings data or central bank buying announcements in the news window. Demand signals absent.

factors.name: Equities stabilizing, risk-on bias fading
factors.summary: S&P 500 and Nasdaq muted Wednesday; Dow scaled new highs. Equity recovery on geopolitical optimism reduces safe-haven demand for gold. Tech sector tumbled while defensives led, suggesting risk appetite is shifting—not fear-driven.

factors.name: Inflation concerns tempered
factors.summary: US-Iran deal optimism has contributed to lower oil and eased inflation expectations (per FXStreet 17:28 'risk appetite shifted to neutral'). No hot inflation surprise in the window; lower oil is disinflationaray.

factors.name: Price action context
factors.summary: Gold plunged to two-month lows near $4,400 (per FXStreet 17:28), with XAU/USD down ~1%+ intraday and -0.59% in 24h. Move is already in progress; no further downside impulse evident yet.

screening.narrative: Gold has sold off sharply to two-month lows ($4,400–$4,440 range) as the US Dollar recovered and geopolitical risk-off sentiment faded on mixed US-Iran deal signals. Trump's explicit rejection of sanctions relief initially spooked equities but ultimately benefited the dollar, while falling oil prices eased inflation expectations and removed a structural bid for gold. The move is already in progress (down ~1% intraday, -0.59% in 24h), suggesting Phase 2 positioning. Risk appetite has shifted from 'fear spike' to 'cautious optimism' on potential Strait of Hormuz normalization, reducing safe-haven demand.
catalysts_ahead: US jobs report (NFP) — likely early June; could reset Fed rate-hike expectations
US CPI/PCE inflation prints — date TBD in next 1–2 weeks; critical for real yields
FOMC meeting outcome (if scheduled within 72h) — any hawkish surprise would weigh further on gold
US-Iran deal finalization or formal breakdown — further resolution would ease geopolitical premium
Treasury yield moves (particularly 10y TIPS) — any fresh decline could support gold bounce
Oil price stabilization/rebound — if Hormuz remains closed longer, oil rally would support inflation hedge demand
screening.risk_warning: If US-Iran negotiations collapse and geopolitical premium returns (Hormuz blockade extends, oil spikes >$110), safe-haven demand and inflation expectations could reverse gold lower bias. Conversely, if real yields (10y TIPS) fall sharply or Fed signals dovishness earlier than expected, gold could breach support and trigger a mean-reversion rally. Late-stage move extension means a sharp pullback to $4,450–$4,480 is plausible before further downside.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already in progress (Phase 2); avoid chasing short here. Better entry on retest of $4,450–$4,470 or if DXY rolls over and real yields decline. Monitor 5-year auction data and upcoming CPI for conviction.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data in news; nominal 10y around 4.18% per 5-year note auction. Real yield direction unclear without inflation expectations confirmation.

factors.name: Fed Policy / FedSpeak
factors.summary: Fed Governor Lisa Cook (27 May, 20:40 ET) explicitly flags readiness to hike rates if inflation persists, citing tariffs, Iran war, and AI investment as price pressures. Hawkish surprise contradicts dovish expectations.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY reversed intraday losses on conflicting Iran headlines (18:19 ET); no clear directional trend yet. USD strength would be bearish for gold but signal remains mixed.

factors.name: Geopolitical Tension – Iran Nuclear Deal
factors.summary: Initial optimism on Iran deal (21:14 ET) knocked down by Trump's rejection of sanctions relief and insistence Strait of Hormuz stay open to all (18:19 ET, 17:52 ET). Risk-off sentiment hurt gold; oil fell >5% after Rubio's dovish comments (19:50 ET).

factors.name: Inflation Momentum & Expectations
factors.summary: Cook flagged oil prices as key inflation risk (20:40 ET); Banxico cut 2026 GDP to 1.1% and warned inflation rising (20:26 ET). Sticky inflation narrative supports Fed hawkishness, headwind for gold.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Brent/WTI)
factors.summary: Crude fell >5% on Rubio's dovish Iran talk (19:50 ET); private inventory draw smaller than expected (21:01 ET). Lower oil eases near-term inflation pressure, reducing safe-haven urgency for gold.

factors.name: Silver (XAG/USD) Relative Weakness
factors.summary: Silver broke 50-day SMA at $75.77 and fell 2.80% to $74.74, targeting $73.00 (19:29 ET). Weakness in silver often precedes gold selling or risk-off, suggesting caution.

factors.name: Risk Sentiment / Equity Action
factors.summary: Tech sector tumbled on earnings; Zscaler -31% (20:36 ET), Salesforce weak guidance (20:57 ET). Defensive posture present, but offset by Fed hawkishness and resolution hopes on Iran reducing crisis premium.

factors.name: Central Bank Gold Buying
factors.summary: No recent PBoC, RBI, or other CB buying signals in news; structural bid remains background support but inactive in this window.

factors.name: Gold ETF Flows (GLD/IAU)
factors.summary: No holdings data provided in news window; cannot assess institutional positioning shifts.

screening.narrative: Gold faces conflicting drivers: hawkish Fed signals (Cook hike warning) and sticky inflation push against safe-haven appeal, while Iran deal hopes and oil weakness trim crisis premium and reduce immediate geopolitical urgency. Silver's breakdown below 50-day SMA signals caution on precious metals broadly. The market is chopping as traders digest whether Fed really will hike (inflation risk) versus whether de-escalation on Iran reduces emergency safe-haven demand. Price action shows modest 4h gain (+0.15%) but 24h loss (-0.46%), suggesting lack of conviction either direction.
catalysts_ahead: BOJ IMES conference (27-28 May, Tokyo): Fed Vice Chair Jefferson & ECB Chief Economist Lane fireside chat on monetary policy, supply shocks — could reset rate expectations
US PCE inflation data (likely early June, pre-scheduled)
Iran nuclear talks update / Trump's next statement — deal clarity or fresh breakdown
Oil inventory data flows — continued easing supports inflation narrative or surprise tightness
Fed speakers continue (Goolsbee, Lombardelli panel on 28 May)
screening.risk_warning: If Fed pivots dovish (e.g., Powell or FOMC signals cuts sooner), real yields collapse and gold surges past $4,510; conversely, if inflation surprises hot or Iran deal collapses and oil spikes, Fed hikes more aggressively, real yields rise, and gold breaks $4,470 support. DXY strength would accelerate downside. Current chop reflects genuine uncertainty on both fronts.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already underway with mixed signals; avoid chasing. Wait for confirmation: if gold reclaims $4,510 on hawkish Fed/inflation confirmation, consider buy; if breaks $4,470, consider short. Better entry after BOJ conference clarifies Fed/ECB divergence or Iran deal resolves.
